title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
What is the Defence Pension Scheme?
The Defence Pension Scheme is a comprehensive retirement benefit framework offered by the Government of India for the retired military personnel of the Armed Forces, including Navy, Army, Air Force, and some Defence civilian employees and their family members in India. Under this scheme, the government provides a monthly pension to the eligible defence personnel after completing qualifying service. The scheme provides regular income and adequate financial support to the ex-army personnel for their financial security post-service. The Defence Pension scheme is governed by the Pension Regulations administered by the Department of Ex-servicemen Welfare under the Ministry of Defence.
Features of the Defence Pension Scheme
Here are some of the salient features of the Pension Scheme for Defence Employees:
Backed by the government1
It is launched by the Government of India and administered by the Ministry of Defence.
Lifetime Pension Security1
The Defence Pension Scheme provides a monthly pension to retired defence personnel, ensuring lifetime financial security post-service.
Family Pension for Dependents1
Eligible family members are granted pension support in the event of the death of the pensioner, enabling them to secure their family income.
Inflation-Linked Dearness Relief (DR)2
Defence pensions are revised periodically through dearness relief to adjust for inflation and rising living costs.
Disability & War Injury Pension
Pensioners invalidated out or injured due to service-related reasons are eligible to receive additional pension benefits.
Beneficiaries1:
The beneficiaries of the Defence Pension Scheme include  personnel below officer rank who have served for a minimum of 15 previous years, former officers who have served for a minimum of 20 previous years, families of the deceased military personnel (spouse or eligible dependants) and civilians employed in the defence sector.
Medical & Healthcare Coverage (ECHS)3
Retired servicemen and their dependents can receive medical benefits such as cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals under the ECHS Scheme (Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme).
Types of Defence Pension Schemes
Here are the several types of Defence Pension Schemes along with their features2:
Benefits of the Defence Pension Scheme
The defence pension scheme is designed specifically for the benefit and well-being of the ex-servicemen of the armed forces. It provides numerous benefits to them and their family. Let us understand some of the benefits of the defence pension scheme:
Regular Income After Retirement2
Pension scheme for Defence Employees ensures a stable and regular income source for the defence personnel and their family members. This steady income flow post-service enables financial security and stability for them and their family.
Inflation Adjusted Income
The Dearness Relief under the scheme is inflation-indexed through periodic adjustments so that pensioners can manage rising costs2.
Medical and Healthcare Benefits
Medical and healthcare benefits such as hospitalisation, treatment cost and other medical costs are provided through the Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme to the personnel and their beneficiaries.
Housing Related Benefits
The scheme also offers several housing advantages, such as allotment of housing units, lands and loans for construction under different schemes- Armed Forces Welfare Housing Organisation (AWHO), Air Force and Navy Housing Boards and Armed Forces Flag Day Fund.
Travel Concessions2
Travel concessions are also available for ex-servicemen and their dependents when travelling by railways or airlines.
Key Takeaways
The defence pension scheme is a retirement benefit framework for retired military personnel of the Armed Forces and some Defence civilian employees and their family members in India. .
The defence pension structure includes service pension, family pension, disability pension, war-injury pension and invalid pension with specific rules governed by the Ministry of Defence’s Pension Regulations.
The scheme includes inflation-adjusted income through the dearness relief
Modern digital administration systems like System for Pension Administration – Raksha (SPARSH) have been introduced in order to streamline the sanction and payment of defence pension in India.
Conclusion
The Defence Pension Scheme is a retirement scheme administered by the Ministry of Defence . It is the Government of India’s commitment and endeavour to safeguard the financial well-being of the armed forces post retirement for their continuous service and contribution to the nation. By providing regular and lifelong pay-out, healthcare assistance, the scheme ensures stability and dignity for the military personnel and their families. The defence pension administration has become smooth and transparent with the implementation of the SPARSH portal, ensuring an efficient, transparent, and pensioner-friendly framework for the disbursement of pension.
FAQs
Who is eligible for a Defence Pension?
Personnel from the Indian Navy, Army, or Air Force who have completed qualifying service, or their family members who are eligible dependents in case of death or disability, are eligible for a defence pension.
How is the amount of my Defence Pension calculated?
Defence pension amount depends on the type of pension payable. For instance, service pension is calculated as 50% of the last drawn salary or the average salary of the last 10 months, whichever is higher and the minimum limit is ₹90002.
What is a PPO (Pension Payment Order)?
A Pension Payment Order is a document that is issued to military personnel and retiring employees4.
How can I access my Defence Pension information online?1
To access your Defence Pension information online, visit SPARSH (System for Pension Administration – Raksha), which has been implemented to streamline pension sanction and disbursal across India for Armed Forces.
Disclaimer
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.

full_pdf_text:
What is the Defence Pension Scheme?
The Defence Pension Scheme is a comprehensive retirement benefit framework offered by the Government of India for the retired military personnel of the Armed Forces, including Navy, Army, Air Force, and some Defence civilian employees and their family members in India. Under this scheme, the government provides a monthly pension to the eligible defence personnel after completing qualifying service. The scheme provides regular income and adequate financial support to the ex-army personnel for their financial security post-service. The Defence Pension scheme is governed by the Pension Regulations administered by the Department of Ex-servicemen Welfare under the Ministry of Defence.
Features of the Defence Pension Scheme
Here are some of the salient features of the Pension Scheme for Defence Employees:
1. Backed by the government1
It is launched by the Government of India and administered by the Ministry of Defence. 2. Lifetime Pension Security1
The Defence Pension Scheme provides a monthly pension to retired defence personnel, ensuring lifetime financial security post-service. 3. Family Pension for Dependents1
Eligible family members are granted pension support in the event of the death of the pensioner, enabling them to secure their family income. 4. Inflation-Linked Dearness Relief (DR)2
Defence pensions are revised periodically through dearness relief to adjust for inflation and rising living costs.
5. Disability & War Injury Pension
Pensioners invalidated out or injured due to service-related reasons are eligible to receive additional pension benefits. 6. Beneficiaries1:
The beneficiaries of the Defence Pension Scheme include personnel below officer rank who have served for a minimum of 15 previous years, former officers who have served for a minimum of 20 previous years, families of the deceased military personnel (spouse or eligible dependants) and civilians employed in the defence sector. 7. Medical & Healthcare Coverage (ECHS)3
Retired servicemen and their dependents can receive medical benefits such as cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals under the ECHS Scheme (Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme).
Types of Defence Pension Schemes
Here are the several types of Defence Pension Schemes along with their features2:
Type of Pension Features Service Pension2
● Minimum Amount- Rs. 9,000
● Maximum Amount is the higher of the following:
⮚ 50% of the last drawn emoluments
⮚ average of emoluments drawn over the previous 10 months
that are reckonable
● Eligibility Requirements: Minimum 15 years of service for personnel below office rank and 20 years of service for Commissioned Officers.
Special Family Pension2
● Special family pension is paid to the personnel’s family at a fixed rate, which is 60% of the last drawn emoluments by the deceased that are reckonable.
● It is applicable if an individual dies and the death can be attributed to or is aggravated by being in the service of the military
Family Pension2 ● Family pension is paid to the family members of the personnel in case of natural death of the personnel.
● The amount of family pension is 30% of the reckonable last drawn emoluments, subject to a minimum of Rs.9,000 per month.
Liberalised Family Pension2
● Liberalised Family Pension is paid to the spouse of soldiers who die due to war or enemy action3.
● The amount of pension is equal to the last drawn salary of the soldier3.
War Injury Pension
● The pension rate for the War Injury Element in case of 100% disability is as follows:
⮚ the reckonable last drawn emoluments if the individual
becomes invalid
⮚ 60% of reckonable last drawn emoluments if the individual is
discharged
⮚ The amount would proportionately reduce if the disability is
below 100%.
Disability Pension2
● Disability Pension is paid to invalided-out cases from the armed forces due to disability that can be attributed to or is aggravated by being in the service of the military.
● It encompasses the Service and Disability Element. The former is 50% of the last drawn emoluments that were reckonable, i.e. the retiring
pension. The latter would be 30% of the last drawn emoluments that are reckonable if the disability is 100%.
● The disability pension is not provided in case of 20% or less disability.
● If the disability severity is lower, the disability element payable is proportionally reduced.
Reservist Pension
● Reservist Pension is paid for at least 15 years in the prescribed and combined Colour and Reserve Service.
● It is 2/3rd of the minimum pension admissible to a Sepoy.
Invalid Pension ● This Pension is paid to Service Personnel who become invalid for service due to any disability that is categorised as Neither Attributable Nor Aggravated (NANA).
● It is applicable to personnel who became invalid from service due to any mental or physical infirmity and were in service on or after 04.01.2019.
Benefits of the Defence Pension Scheme
The defence pension scheme is designed specifically for the benefit and well-being of the ex- servicemen of the armed forces. It provides numerous benefits to them and their family. Let us understand some of the benefits of the defence pension scheme:
1. Regular Income After Retirement2
Pension scheme for Defence Employees ensures a stable and regular income source for the defence personnel and their family members. This steady income flow post-service enables financial security and stability for them and their family.
2. Inflation Adjusted Income
The Dearness Relief under the scheme is inflation-indexed through periodic adjustments so that pensioners can manage rising costs2.
3. Medical and Healthcare Benefits
Medical and healthcare benefits such as hospitalisation, treatment cost and other medical costs are provided through the Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme to the personnel and their beneficiaries. 4. Housing Related Benefits
The scheme also offers several housing advantages, such as allotment of housing units, lands and loans for construction under different schemes- Armed Forces Welfare Housing Organisation (AWHO), Air Force and Navy Housing Boards and Armed Forces Flag Day Fund.
5. Travel Concessions2
Travel concessions are also available for ex-servicemen and their dependents when travelling by railways or airlines. Key Takeaways
1. The defence pension scheme is a retirement benefit framework for retired military
personnel of the Armed Forces and some Defence civilian employees and their family members in India. .
2. The defence pension structure includes service pension, family pension, disability pension,
war-injury pension and invalid pension with specific rules governed by the Ministry of Defence’s Pension Regulations.
3. The scheme includes inflation-adjusted income through the dearness relief
4. Modern digital administration systems like System for Pension Administration – Raksha
(SPARSH) have been introduced in order to streamline the sanction and payment of defence pension in India.
Conclusion
The Defence Pension Scheme is a retirement scheme administered by the Ministry of Defence . It is the Government of India’s commitment and endeavour to safeguard the financial well-being of the armed forces post retirement for their continuous service and contribution to the nation. By providing regular and lifelong pay-out, healthcare assistance, the scheme ensures stability and dignity for the military personnel and their families. The defence pension administration has become smooth and transparent with the implementation of the SPARSH portal, ensuring an efficient, transparent, and pensioner-friendly framework for the disbursement of pension.
FAQs
1. Who is eligible for a Defence Pension?
Personnel from the Indian Navy, Army, or Air Force who have completed qualifying service, or their family members who are eligible dependents in case of death or disability, are eligible for a defence pension. 2. How is the amount of my Defence Pension calculated?
Defence pension amount depends on the type of pension payable. For instance, service pension is calculated as 50% of the last drawn salary or the average salary of the last 10 months, whichever is higher and the minimum limit is ₹90002. 3. What is a PPO (Pension Payment Order)?
A Pension Payment Order is a document that is issued to military personnel and retiring employees4. 4. How can I access my Defence Pension information online?1
To access your Defence Pension information online, visit SPARSH (System for Pension Administration – Raksha), which has been implemented to streamline pension sanction and disbursal across India for Armed Forces.
Disclaimer

While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.

guide-for-ex-servicemen-and-families/

● Special family pension is paid to the personnel’s family at a fixed rate, which is 60% of the last drawn emoluments by the deceased that are reckonable.
● It is applicable if an individual dies and the death can be attributed to or is aggravated by being in the service of the military
Family Pension2 ● Family pension is paid to the family members of the personnel in case of natural death of the personnel.
● The amount of family pension is 30% of the reckonable last drawn emoluments, subject to a minimum of Rs.9,000 per month.

● Liberalised Family Pension is paid to the spouse of soldiers who die due to war or enemy action3.

● The pension rate for the War Injury Element in case of 100% disability is as follows:
⮚ the reckonable last drawn emoluments if the individual
becomes invalid
⮚ 60% of reckonable last drawn emoluments if the individual is
discharged
⮚ The amount would proportionately reduce if the disability is
below 100%.
Disability Pension2
● Disability Pension is paid to invalided-out cases from the armed forces due to disability that can be attributed to or is aggravated by being in the service of the military.
● It encompasses the Service and Disability Element.
The former is 50% of the last drawn emoluments that were reckonable, i.e. the retiring
pension.
The latter would be 30% of the last drawn emoluments that are reckonable if the disability is 100%.
● The disability pension is not provided in case of 20% or less disability.
● If the disability severity is lower, the disability element payable is proportionally reduced.
Reservist Pension
● Reservist Pension is paid for at least 15 years in the prescribed and combined Colour and Reserve Service.
● It is 2/3rd of the minimum pension admissible to a Sepoy.
Invalid Pension ● This Pension is paid to Service Personnel who become invalid for service due to any disability that is categorised as Neither Attributable Nor Aggravated (NANA).
● It is applicable to personnel who became invalid from service due to any mental or physical infirmity and were in service on or after 04.01.2019.

changes.pdf_final: Modern digital administration systems like System for Pension Administration – Raksha
(SPARSH) have been introduced in order to streamline the sanction and payment of defence pension in India.

changes.word_original: Personnel from the Indian Navy, Army, or Air Force who have completed qualifying service, or their family members who are eligible dependents in case of death or disability, are eligible for a defence pension.
changes.pdf_final: Personnel from the Indian Navy, Army, or Air Force who have completed qualifying service, or their family members who are eligible dependents in case of death or disability, are eligible for a defence pension. 2.

changes.word_original: For instance, service pension is calculated as 50% of the last drawn salary or the average salary of the last 10 months, whichever is higher and the minimum limit is ₹90002.
changes.pdf_final: For instance, service pension is calculated as 50% of the last drawn salary or the average salary of the last 10 months, whichever is higher and the minimum limit is ₹90002. 3.

changes.word_original: A Pension Payment Order is a document that is issued to military personnel and retiring employees4.
changes.pdf_final: A Pension Payment Order is a document that is issued to military personnel and retiring employees4. 4.
